element_extraction_methodology: Each element represents one discrete obligation or competency requirement extracted from articles that impose substantive workforce-relevant requirements. Elements are categorized as 'competency' (article directly states a workforce competency, training, or literacy requirement) or 'obligation' (article states a legal requirement whose fulfillment requires identifiable workforce competencies). Articles covering institutional governance, penalties, amendments to other legislation, and transitional provisions are excluded as non-workforce-relevant. Element text preserves the original regulatory language; competency_implication field provides the workforce competency interpretation used for scoring.
scoring_mode_note: All elements scored via Cross-Encoder STS as primary (consistent with STRM-001 through STRM-004). The competency_implication field bridges the vocabulary gap between legal prose and competency language, making STS appropriate for both element types. NLI entailment was initially planned as primary for obligation elements but diagnostic scoring showed near-zero signal (mean 0.007). Rationale type is Functional per NIST IR 8477. See ADR-019 for full methodology documentation.

elements.obligation_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall take measures to ensure, to their best extent, a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems on their behalf, taking into account their technical knowledge, experience, education and training and the context the AI systems are to be used in, and considering the persons or groups of persons on whom the AI systems are to be used.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI literacy program design, including assessment of staff technical knowledge, experience, education and training needs, contextual adaptation of AI literacy content to operational contexts, and consideration of affected populations in AI literacy planning.

elements.obligation_text: A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI risk management system design, including establishment, implementation, documentation, and maintenance of risk management processes specific to AI systems throughout the system lifecycle.

elements.obligation_text: The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic review and updating. It shall comprise: the identification and analysis of known and reasonably foreseeable risks; the estimation and evaluation of risks under intended use and reasonably foreseeable misuse; the evaluation of other risks based on post-market monitoring data; and the adoption of appropriate and targeted risk management measures.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in conducting iterative AI risk assessment across the system lifecycle, including risk identification and analysis, risk estimation under intended use and foreseeable misuse conditions, integration of post-market monitoring data into risk evaluation, and design of targeted risk management measures.

elements.obligation_text: The risk management measures shall give due consideration to the effects and possible interactions resulting from the combined application of the requirements set out in this Section, with a view to minimising risks more effectively while achieving an appropriate balance in implementing the measures to fulfil those requirements.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to assess interaction effects between multiple AI system requirements and design balanced risk management measures that address combined risks while maintaining regulatory compliance across all applicable requirements.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be tested for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose and that they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing and executing test protocols for high-risk AI systems, including identification of appropriate risk management measures through testing, consistency verification for intended purpose, and compliance testing against regulatory requirements.

elements.obligation_text: Testing procedures may include testing in real-world conditions. Testing in real-world conditions shall not be a condition for placing the high-risk AI system on the market or putting it into service. The testing of high-risk AI systems shall be performed at any point throughout the development process, and, in any event, prior to their being placed on the market or put into service.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system testing methodologies including real-world testing protocols, pre-market testing requirements, and integration of testing throughout the development lifecycle.

elements.obligation_text: When implementing the risk management system, providers of high-risk AI systems shall give consideration to whether the high-risk AI system is likely to adversely impact persons under the age of 18 or, as appropriate, other vulnerable groups.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of vulnerability assessment for AI systems, including methods for evaluating adverse impacts on minors and other vulnerable groups, and integration of vulnerability considerations into risk management processes.

elements.obligation_text: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate for the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system. Those practices shall concern: relevant design choices; data collection processes and the origin of data; relevant data-preparation processing operations such as annotation, labelling, cleaning, updating, enrichment and aggregation; the formulation of assumptions about the information the data are supposed to measure and represent; an assessment of the availability, quantity and suitability of the data sets that are needed; examination for possible biases likely to affect health and safety or fundamental rights; appropriate measures to detect, prevent and mitigate possible biases; the identification of relevant data gaps or shortcomings.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI training data governance, including design choice documentation, data provenance tracking, data preparation operations management, assumption formulation and documentation, data adequacy assessment, bias detection and mitigation, and data gap identification for high-risk AI systems.

elements.obligation_text: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be relevant, sufficiently representative, and to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete in view of the intended purpose. They shall have the appropriate statistical properties, including, where applicable, as regards the persons or groups of persons in relation to whom the high-risk AI system is intended to be used.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of statistical quality requirements for AI training data, including representativeness assessment, error analysis, completeness evaluation, and demographic statistical properties relevant to intended use populations.

elements.obligation_text: To the extent that it is strictly necessary for the purpose of ensuring bias detection and correction in relation to the high-risk AI systems, the providers of such systems may exceptionally process special categories of personal data, subject to appropriate safeguards for the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of lawful processing of special category personal data for AI bias detection, including legal basis assessment, necessity evaluation, safeguard implementation for fundamental rights, and documentation requirements under GDPR Article 9 and AI Act interaction.

elements.obligation_text: The technical documentation of a high-risk AI system shall be drawn up before that system is placed on the market or put into service and shall be kept up-to-date. The technical documentation shall be drawn up in such a way as to demonstrate that the high-risk AI system complies with the requirements set out in this Section and to provide national competent authorities and notified bodies with the necessary information in a clear and comprehensive form to assess the compliance of the AI system with those requirements.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in creating and maintaining technical documentation for AI systems that demonstrates regulatory compliance, including structuring documentation for national authority and notified body assessment, maintaining currency through the system lifecycle, and mapping documentation to specific regulatory requirements.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system logging architecture, including design of automatic event recording mechanisms, log retention over system lifetime, and technical implementation of audit trail capabilities.

elements.obligation_text: In order to facilitate the monitoring of the functioning of the high-risk AI system, the logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for: identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk; facilitating the post-market monitoring; and monitoring the operation of the high-risk AI system.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing AI monitoring and logging systems that capture risk-relevant events, support post-market monitoring workflows, and enable operational monitoring of high-risk AI systems.

elements.article_title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use it appropriately.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system transparency design, including interpretability mechanisms, output explanation methods, and design approaches that enable deployers to appropriately interpret and use AI system outputs.

elements.article_title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be accompanied by instructions for use in an appropriate digital format or otherwise that include concise, complete, correct and clear information that is relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in creating AI system usage documentation that meets regulatory standards for completeness, correctness, clarity, relevance, accessibility, and comprehensibility for deployer audiences.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of human-machine interface design for AI oversight, including design of effective oversight mechanisms, human-in-the-loop architectures, and interface tools that enable natural persons to exercise meaningful oversight during AI system operation.

elements.obligation_text: The measures for human oversight shall be commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system, and shall ensure that the natural persons to whom human oversight is assigned: are able to properly understand the relevant capacities and limitations of the high-risk AI system; are able to duly monitor its operation; remain aware of the possible tendency of automatically relying on the output produced by a high-risk AI system; are able to correctly interpret the high-risk AI system's output; and are able to decide not to use the high-risk AI system, to disregard, override or reverse its output.
elements.competency_implication: Ability to exercise human oversight of AI systems, including understanding system capacities and limitations, monitoring system operation, recognizing automation bias, correctly interpreting AI outputs, and exercising authority to override or reverse AI system decisions.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system quality engineering, including accuracy measurement and benchmarking, robustness testing and assurance, cybersecurity integration for AI systems, and lifecycle performance consistency monitoring.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against attempts by unauthorised third parties to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting system vulnerabilities. The technical solutions to address AI specific vulnerabilities shall include, where appropriate, measures to prevent, detect, respond to, resolve and control for attacks trying to manipulate the training data set (data poisoning), or pre-trained components used in training (model poisoning), inputs designed to cause the AI model to make a mistake (adversarial examples or model evasion), confidentiality attacks or model flaws.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI-specific cybersecurity, including defense against data poisoning, model poisoning, adversarial examples, model evasion, confidentiality attacks, and model flaw exploitation — encompassing prevention, detection, response, resolution, and control measures for AI system vulnerabilities.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall: ensure that their high-risk AI systems are compliant with the requirements set out in Section 2; have a quality management system in place which complies with Article 17; keep the documentation referred to in Article 18.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of high-risk AI system compliance management, including regulatory requirement tracking, quality management system implementation, and documentation management for AI system providers.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall: ensure that the high-risk AI system undergoes the relevant conformity assessment procedure prior to placing on market or putting into service; draw up an EU declaration of conformity; affix the CE marking; upon request of a national competent authority, demonstrate the conformity of the high-risk AI system; and ensure that the AI system is in compliance with accessibility requirements.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system conformity assessment execution, including selection and management of appropriate conformity assessment procedures, EU declaration of conformity preparation, CE marking processes, demonstration of compliance to national authorities, and accessibility compliance.

elements.obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems shall put a quality management system in place that ensures compliance with this Regulation. That system shall be documented in a systematic and orderly manner in the form of written policies, procedures and instructions, and shall include at a minimum: a strategy for regulatory compliance; techniques, procedures and systematic actions to be used for the design, design control and design verification of the high-risk AI system; techniques, procedures and systematic actions to be used for the development, quality control and quality assurance of the high-risk AI system; examination, test and validation procedures to be carried out before, during and after the development of the high-risk AI system; technical specifications; and systems and procedures for data management.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing and implementing AI quality management systems, including regulatory compliance strategy, design control and verification procedures, development quality control, examination and validation procedures throughout the AI lifecycle, technical specification management, and data management systems.

elements.obligation_text: The quality management system shall also include: systems and procedures for data management including data acquisition, data collection, data analysis, data labelling, data storage, data filtration, data mining, data aggregation, data retention and any other operation regarding the data; the risk management system; the setting-up, implementation and maintenance of a post-market monitoring system; procedures related to the reporting of a serious incident; the handling of communication with national competent authorities, other relevant authorities, notified bodies, other operators, customers, or other interested parties; and systems and procedures for record keeping of all relevant documentation and information.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of comprehensive AI data management systems including full data lifecycle operations, integration with risk management systems, post-market monitoring system design, serious incident reporting procedures, regulatory authority communication protocols, and documentation and record-keeping systems.

elements.obligation_text: The quality management system shall include: resource management, including human resources-related measures.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of human resource management for AI quality management, including competency requirements, training programs, and resource allocation for AI system development and compliance.

elements.obligation_text: The quality management system shall include: an accountability framework setting out the responsibilities of the management and other staff with regard to all aspects listed in this paragraph; and a corrective actions and corrective action procedures procedure.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing AI governance accountability frameworks, including responsibility assignment for management and staff across all quality management system aspects, and corrective action procedure design and implementation.

elements.article_title: Corrective actions and duty of information
elements.obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems which consider or have reason to consider that a high-risk AI system that they have placed on the market or put into service is not in conformity with this Regulation shall immediately take the necessary corrective actions to bring that system into conformity, to withdraw it or to recall it, as appropriate.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system non-conformity response, including conformity monitoring, corrective action decision-making, system withdrawal and recall procedures, and regulatory notification processes.

elements.obligation_text: The authorised representative shall perform the tasks specified in the mandate received from the provider. It shall provide a copy of the mandate to the market surveillance authority upon request. The mandate shall empower the authorised representative to: verify that the EU declaration of conformity and the technical documentation have been drawn up; keep available the documentation for national competent authorities and authorities referred to in Article 74(10); cooperate with competent authorities on any action the latter takes in relation to the high-risk AI system.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of EU AI Act authorized representative responsibilities, including conformity documentation verification, documentation availability management, and regulatory authority cooperation procedures for high-risk AI systems.

elements.obligation_text: Before placing a high-risk AI system on the market, importers shall ensure that: the appropriate conformity assessment procedure has been carried out by the provider; the provider has drawn up the technical documentation; the system bears the required CE marking and is accompanied by the EU declaration of conformity and instructions for use.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system import compliance requirements, including verification of provider conformity assessment, technical documentation assessment, CE marking verification, and declaration of conformity and instructions review.

elements.obligation_text: Before making a high-risk AI system available on the market, distributors shall verify that the high-risk AI system bears the required CE marking, that it is accompanied by the required EU declaration of conformity and instructions for use, and that the provider and the importer have complied with their respective obligations.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system distribution compliance, including CE marking verification, documentation compliance checking, and supply chain compliance verification for provider and importer obligations.

elements.article_title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
elements.obligation_text: Under the conditions laid down in this Article, a distributor, importer, deployer, or other third party shall be considered to be a provider of a high-risk AI system and shall be subject to the obligations of the provider if they: put their name or trademark on a high-risk AI system already placed on the market or put into service; make a substantial modification to a high-risk AI system; or modify the intended purpose of an AI system which is not classified as high-risk, resulting in it becoming high-risk.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI value chain liability triggers, including conditions under which rebranding, substantial modification, or intended purpose modification transfers provider obligations to other value chain actors.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers shall assign human oversight to natural persons who have the necessary competence, training and authority, as well as the necessary support.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing human oversight assignments for high-risk AI systems, including competency assessment of oversight personnel, training program design, authority delegation frameworks, and support structure provision.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall take appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure they use such systems in accordance with the instructions for use accompanying the systems.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of technical and organisational measures for AI system deployment, including instructions-for-use compliance, operational procedure design, and deployment configuration management.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: To the extent the deployer exercises control over the input data, that deployer shall ensure that input data is relevant and sufficiently representative in view of the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI input data quality management, including relevance assessment, representativeness evaluation for intended purpose, and data quality assurance for deployer-controlled inputs.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers shall monitor the operation of the high-risk AI system on the basis of the instructions for use and, where relevant, inform providers. Where deployers have reason to consider that the use of the high-risk AI system in accordance with the instructions may result in that AI system presenting a risk, they shall, without undue delay, inform the provider or distributor and the relevant market surveillance authority and shall suspend the use of that system.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system operational monitoring, including performance monitoring against instructions for use, risk signal identification, provider notification procedures, market surveillance authority reporting, and system suspension decision-making.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall keep the logs automatically generated by that high-risk AI system to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months, unless provided otherwise in applicable Union or national law.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system log management requirements, including log retention policies, control assessment, retention period determination based on intended purpose, and compliance with applicable Union and national law retention requirements.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Before putting into service or using a high-risk AI system at the workplace, deployers who are employers shall inform workers' representatives and the affected workers that they will be subject to the use of the high-risk AI system.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of workplace AI deployment notification requirements, including worker representative consultation procedures, affected worker notification, and employment law compliance for AI system deployment.

elements.article_title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall use the information provided under Article 13 to comply with their obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment under Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 or Article 27 of Directive (EU) 2016/680.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of data protection impact assessment for AI systems, including integration of AI Act transparency information with GDPR DPIA requirements, and coordination of AI system deployment with data protection compliance.

elements.obligation_text: Before deploying a high-risk AI system, deployers that are bodies governed by public law, or are private entities providing public services, and deployers of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 5(b) and 5(c) of Annex III shall perform an assessment of the impact on fundamental rights that the use of such system may produce.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in conducting fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including methodology selection, fundamental rights identification, impact evaluation, and assessment documentation for both public and private entities.

elements.obligation_text: The fundamental rights impact assessment shall include: a description of the deployer's processes in which the high-risk AI system will be used; a description of the period of time within which, and the frequency with which, each high-risk AI system is intended to be used; the categories of natural persons and groups likely to be affected; the specific risks of harm likely to impact the identified categories of persons or groups; a description of the implementation of human oversight measures; the measures to be taken where those risks materialise.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of fundamental rights impact assessment content requirements, including process documentation, use case scoping, affected population identification, harm risk assessment, human oversight implementation planning, and risk mitigation measure design.

elements.obligation_text: For high-risk AI systems, where the provider has applied harmonised standards or common specifications, the provider shall opt for a conformity assessment procedure based on: the internal control referred to in Annex VI; or the assessment of the quality management system and the assessment of the technical documentation with the involvement of a notified body referred to in Annex VII.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI conformity assessment procedures, including internal control assessment methodology (Annex VI), notified body assessment processes (Annex VII), harmonised standard application, and common specification compliance demonstration.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems that have already been subject to a conformity assessment procedure shall undergo a new conformity assessment procedure in the event of a substantial modification, regardless of whether the modified system is intended to be further distributed or continues to be used by the current deployer.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of substantial modification assessment for AI systems, including criteria for determining when modifications trigger re-assessment, conformity assessment procedure selection for modified systems, and change management processes.

elements.obligation_text: Before placing on the market or putting into service a high-risk AI system, the provider or, where applicable, the authorised representative shall register themselves and their system in the EU database referred to in Article 71.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of EU AI database registration requirements, including provider registration procedures, system registration data requirements, and pre-market registration compliance.

elements.obligation_text: Deployers who are public authorities or Union institutions, bodies, offices or agencies shall register and select the system and register its use in the EU database. Other deployers shall be authorised to register themselves and select the system and register its use.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of deployer registration obligations in the EU AI database, including system selection documentation, use case registration, and differentiated requirements for public authorities versus other deployers.

elements.article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Providers shall ensure that AI systems intended to interact directly with natural persons are designed and developed in such a way that the natural persons concerned are informed that they are interacting with an AI system, unless this is obvious from the point of view of a natural person who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system interaction disclosure requirements, including design of notification mechanisms, assessment of when disclosure is obvious, and implementation of transparency measures for human-AI interaction.

elements.article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in implementing AI content provenance marking, including machine-readable format selection for synthetic content identification, content authenticity metadata design, and detection mechanism implementation for artificially generated or manipulated content.

elements.article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers of an emotion recognition system or a biometric categorisation system shall inform the natural persons exposed thereto of the operation of the system, and shall process the personal data in accordance with Regulations (EU) 2016/679 and (EU) 2018/1725 and Directive (EU) 2016/680.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of emotion recognition and biometric categorisation disclosure requirements, including affected person notification procedures and coordination with GDPR personal data processing requirements.

elements.article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
elements.obligation_text: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated. Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text which is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest shall disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of deepfake and AI-generated content disclosure requirements, including identification of content subject to disclosure obligations, disclosure mechanism design, and public interest content transparency requirements.

elements.article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall draw up and keep up-to-date the technical documentation of the model, including its training and testing process and the results of its evaluation, which shall contain, at a minimum, the information set out in Annex XI.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in creating and maintaining technical documentation for general-purpose AI models, including training process documentation, testing methodology documentation, evaluation results recording, and Annex XI compliance.

elements.article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall draw up, keep up-to-date and make available information and documentation to providers of AI systems who intend to integrate the general-purpose AI model into their AI systems, to enable them to have a good understanding of the capabilities and limitations of the general-purpose AI model and to comply with their obligations.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in creating downstream integration documentation for general-purpose AI models, including capability and limitation disclosure, integration guidance for AI system providers, and documentation that enables downstream regulatory compliance.

elements.article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall put in place a policy to comply with Union law on copyright and related rights, and in particular to identify and comply with a reservation of rights expressed pursuant to Article 4(3) of Directive (EU) 2019/790.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of copyright compliance for AI model training, including policy development for Union copyright law, rights reservation identification and compliance, and text and data mining exception management under Directive 2019/790.

elements.article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall draw up and make publicly available a sufficiently detailed summary about the content used for training of the general-purpose AI model, according to a template provided by the AI Office.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in creating AI training data transparency disclosures, including training content summarisation, compliance with AI Office template requirements, and public disclosure management.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall perform model evaluation in accordance with standardised protocols and tools reflecting the state of the art, including conducting and documenting adversarial testing of the model with a view to identifying and mitigating systemic risks.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI model evaluation and adversarial testing for systemic risk, including standardised evaluation protocol execution, adversarial testing methodology, systemic risk identification and documentation, and state-of-the-art testing tool application.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall assess and mitigate possible systemic risks, including their sources, that may stem from the development, the placing on the market, or the use of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of systemic AI risk assessment, including risk source identification across development, market placement, and deployment phases, systemic risk evaluation methodologies, and mitigation strategy design for general-purpose AI models.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall keep track of, document, and report without undue delay to the AI Office and, as appropriate, to national competent authorities, relevant information about serious incidents and possible corrective measures to address them.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI serious incident management and regulatory reporting, including incident tracking systems, documentation procedures, AI Office and national authority reporting protocols, and corrective measure identification and reporting.

elements.article_title: Obligations of providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
elements.obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk shall ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity protection for the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk and the physical infrastructure of the model.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of cybersecurity requirements for GPAI models with systemic risk, including model security architecture, physical infrastructure protection, and adequate cybersecurity level determination.

elements.obligation_text: Providers shall establish and document a post-market monitoring system in a manner that is proportionate to the nature of the AI technologies and the risks of the high-risk AI system.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in designing AI post-market monitoring systems, including proportionality assessment for AI technologies, monitoring system architecture, and documentation of monitoring approaches appropriate to risk levels.

elements.obligation_text: The post-market monitoring system shall actively and systematically collect, document and analyse relevant data which may be provided by deployers or which may be collected through other sources, on the performance of high-risk AI systems throughout their lifetime, and which allow the provider to evaluate the continuous compliance of AI systems with the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI performance data collection and analysis for post-market surveillance, including deployer data integration, multi-source data aggregation, lifetime performance analysis, and continuous compliance evaluation against regulatory requirements.

elements.obligation_text: Providers of high-risk AI systems placed on the Union market shall report any serious incident to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States where that incident occurred.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI serious incident reporting requirements, including serious incident identification criteria, market surveillance authority notification procedures, Member State jurisdiction determination, and reporting timeline compliance.

elements.obligation_text: The provider shall, following the reporting of a serious incident, without undue delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI serious incident investigation, including root cause analysis, risk assessment of incidents, AI system investigation methodology, and corrective action design and implementation following serious incidents.

elements.obligation_text: The placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of AI systems that deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective or the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a group of persons, is prohibited.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of prohibited AI practices under EU law, including identification of subliminal techniques, manipulative and deceptive AI system characteristics, and assessment of whether AI systems materially distort behaviour in prohibited ways.

elements.article_title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
elements.obligation_text: An AI system listed in Annex III shall be considered to be high-risk where it poses a significant risk of harm to the health, safety or fundamental rights of natural persons. A provider who considers that an AI system referred to in Annex III is not high-risk shall document its assessment before that system is placed on the market or put into service.
elements.competency_implication: Knowledge of AI system risk classification methodology, including Annex III high-risk category assessment, significant risk determination criteria, and documentation requirements for non-high-risk classification decisions.

elements.obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against errors, faults or inconsistencies that may occur within the system or the environment in which the system operates, in particular due to their interaction with natural persons or other systems. Technical redundancy solutions shall include, where appropriate, backup or fail-safe plans.
elements.competency_implication: Skill in AI system resilience engineering, including error handling design, fault tolerance implementation, environmental interaction robustness, human-AI interaction failure modes, system-to-system interaction resilience, and backup and fail-safe plan design.
